source_paper_title: On the Sequential Convergence of Lloyd’s Algorithms
context:
Page 3, Section 1 (Introduction), paragraph discussing prior work on accumulation points and sequential convergence

Beyond existing results, a general sequential convergence guaranty for Lloyd’s methods in the semi-discrete setting remains open.
title: Sequential convergence of Lloyd’s method for semi-discrete quantization without definability/analyticity assumptions
background_latex:
- Let \(d\in\mathbb{N}^*\) and \(N\in\mathbb{N}^*\).
- Let \(\mu\) be a probability measure on \(\mathbb{R}^d\) that is absolutely continuous with respect to Lebesgue measure, with compact support \(\mathrm{Supp}\,\mu\subset \mathbb{R}^d\), and with density \(f\).
- For a point cloud \(Y=(y_1,\dots,y_N)\in(\mathbb{R}^d)^N\), define the generalized diagonal \(D_N:=\{Y\in(\mathbb{R}^d)^N:\exists i\neq j,\ y_i=y_j\}\).
- Define the optimal quantization functional
\[
G_N(Y):=\frac12\int_{\mathbb{R}^d}\min_{i\in\{1,\dots,N\}}\|x-y_i\|^2\,f(x)\,dx,
\]
and its Lloyd (Voronoi-barycentric) fixed-point iteration \(Y_{n+1}=T_N(Y_n)\) (well-defined for \(Y_n\notin D_N\)), where \(T_N(Y)\) has components
\[
(T_N(Y))_i:=\frac{\int_{V_i(Y)} x\,d\mu(x)}{\mu(V_i(Y))},\qquad i=1,\dots,N,
\]
with Voronoi cells \(V_i(Y):=\{x\in\mathbb{R}^d:\|x-y_i\|^2<\|x-y_j\|^2\ \forall j\neq i\}\).
- Define the uniform quantization functional
\[
F_N(Y):=\frac12\,W_2^2\Bigl(\mu,\frac1N\sum_{i=1}^N\delta_{y_i}\Bigr),
\]
where \(W_2\) is the 2-Wasserstein distance for the squared Euclidean cost.
- Consider the corresponding Lloyd-type iteration for uniform quantization \(Y_{n+1}=B_N(Y_n)\) defined via Laguerre (power) cells (so that each cell has \(\mu\)-mass \(1/N\)) and barycentric updates (well-defined for \(Y_n\notin D_N\)).
problem_definition_latex:
Prove (or disprove, by counterexample) that for general absolutely continuous compactly supported target measures \(\mu\) (with no additional rigidity assumptions such as analyticity or global subanalyticity/definability of the density \(f\)), the sequences \((Y_n)_{n\ge 0}\) generated by Lloyd’s fixed-point iterations for
- optimal quantization: \(Y_{n+1}=T_N(Y_n)\) applied to \(G_N\), and/or
- uniform quantization: \(Y_{n+1}=B_N(Y_n)\) applied to \(F_N\),
are sequentially convergent, i.e. converge to a single accumulation point (equivalently, do not exhibit cycling/chaotic behavior), and that any such limit point is a critical point of the corresponding functional \(G_N\) or \(F_N\).
latex:
- Let \(d\in\mathbb{N}^*\) and \(N\in\mathbb{N}^*\).
- Let \(\mu\) be a probability measure on \(\mathbb{R}^d\) that is absolutely continuous with respect to Lebesgue measure, with compact support \(\mathrm{Supp}\,\mu\subset \mathbb{R}^d\), and with density \(f\).
- For a point cloud \(Y=(y_1,\dots,y_N)\in(\mathbb{R}^d)^N\), define the generalized diagonal \(D_N:=\{Y\in(\mathbb{R}^d)^N:\exists i\neq j,\ y_i=y_j\}\).
- Define the optimal quantization functional
\[
G_N(Y):=\frac12\int_{\mathbb{R}^d}\min_{i\in\{1,\dots,N\}}\|x-y_i\|^2\,f(x)\,dx,
\]
and its Lloyd (Voronoi-barycentric) fixed-point iteration \(Y_{n+1}=T_N(Y_n)\) (well-defined for \(Y_n\notin D_N\)), where \(T_N(Y)\) has components
\[
(T_N(Y))_i:=\frac{\int_{V_i(Y)} x\,d\mu(x)}{\mu(V_i(Y))},\qquad i=1,\dots,N,
\]
with Voronoi cells \(V_i(Y):=\{x\in\mathbb{R}^d:\|x-y_i\|^2<\|x-y_j\|^2\ \forall j\neq i\}\).
- Define the uniform quantization functional
\[
F_N(Y):=\frac12\,W_2^2\Bigl(\mu,\frac1N\sum_{i=1}^N\delta_{y_i}\Bigr),
\]
where \(W_2\) is the 2-Wasserstein distance for the squared Euclidean cost.
- Consider the corresponding Lloyd-type iteration for uniform quantization \(Y_{n+1}=B_N(Y_n)\) defined via Laguerre (power) cells (so that each cell has \(\mu\)-mass \(1/N\)) and barycentric updates (well-defined for \(Y_n\notin D_N\)).

Prove (or disprove, by counterexample) that for general absolutely continuous compactly supported target measures \(\mu\) (with no additional rigidity assumptions such as analyticity or global subanalyticity/definability of the density \(f\)), the sequences \((Y_n)_{n\ge 0}\) generated by Lloyd’s fixed-point iterations for
- optimal quantization: \(Y_{n+1}=T_N(Y_n)\) applied to \(G_N\), and/or
- uniform quantization: \(Y_{n+1}=B_N(Y_n)\) applied to \(F_N\),
are sequentially convergent, i.e. converge to a single accumulation point (equivalently, do not exhibit cycling/chaotic behavior), and that any such limit point is a critical point of the corresponding functional \(G_N\) or \(F_N\).
